history.hypothesis: AMD and other high-beta semiconductors exhibit explosive range expansions that can be captured with ATR-based Keltner Channel breakouts. By requiring ROC momentum confirmation and volume spikes, we filter out false breakouts. Tight ATR trailing stops (2.5x) limit drawdown on volatile whipsaws while letting winners run. The 50 EMA trend filter ensures we only trade in the direction of the broader trend.

The ATR trailing stop (2.5x) is the primary drawdown culprit — when breakouts fail, too much loss accumulates before exit. Switching to Donchian Channel breakout with midpoint exit (like the proven volume_breakout pattern that achieved 8.6% drawdown) will cut losses faster. To address the low trade count (7 vs 15 minimum), I'm shortening the Donchian period to 15, lowering volume threshold to 1.2x, and using a shorter 30 EMA trend filter to allow more entries while still filtering noise.
